preset_description: Dark = substance signal. Spotlights product capabilities and metrics. CTA and pricing are understated — features are the story, not conversion pressure.

brief.audience: Startups needing an AI-ready design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context immediately: positions MODE as the AI-ready design system built for startups, anchoring the Validator visitor with a clear understanding of what this product is and confirming they're in the right place at the decision stage.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators need trust signals before they commit. Placing social proof, customer logos, and authority markers early satisfies their instinct to verify legitimacy and reduces skepticism before diving into harder proof points.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required by both the decision funnel stage and the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study data, and quantified outcomes from startups using MODE give the Validator the empirical proof they demand before making a purchasing decision.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents pricing tiers, plan comparisons, and clear choice framing so the startup can evaluate which plan fits their needs — positioned after evidence so the value has been substantiated before price is introduced.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: The commitment prompt and primary CTA placed immediately before Recovery as required. With credibility verified and evidence reviewed, the Validator is now primed to act — a direct, confident CTA to convert to paid.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths such as contacting sales, exploring docs, or comparing plans further — catching any Validator who still has lingering questions or isn't ready to commit on this visit.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes context immediately: positions MODE as the AI-ready design system built for startups, anchoring the Validator visitor with a clear understanding of what this product is and confirming they're in the right place at the decision stage.

sections.rationale: Required by both the decision funnel stage and the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study data, and quantified outcomes from startups using MODE give the Validator the empirical proof they demand before making a purchasing decision.

sections.name: Pricing
sections.rationale: Required at the decision funnel stage. Presents pricing tiers, plan comparisons, and clear choice framing so the startup can evaluate which plan fits their needs — positioned after evidence so the value has been substantiated before price is introduced.

sections.rationale: The commitment prompt and primary CTA placed immediately before Recovery as required. With credibility verified and evidence reviewed, the Validator is now primed to act — a direct, confident CTA to convert to paid.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages — never suppress it.

For decision stage: centered offer presentation with price_display slot surfacing the price as a fact before the CTA — use when product-context.json has pricing data and the goal is to anchor the commitment. Validator variant always includes trust_signal.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: A semantic resolution engine that makes components context-aware — by archetype, funnel stage, and intent state. No manual variants. No configuration debt.
slots.trust_signal: Lifetime access. No subscription. No runtime dependency.

page.reasoning: Primary trust signal for Validator archetype. Always include role and company for Validator credibility — with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity. Place near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.

slots.quote: We were generating landing pages with AI and getting structurally correct but tonally incoherent output. MODE was the missing layer — it resolved copy density, CTA pressure, and visual emphasis to the archetype automatically. That's not a feature, that's a different mental model.

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust.

slots.headline: What teams measure after MODE is in the stack.

stats.label: Reduction in manual variant work per page build
Median time from install to first fully resolved page output
Parallel archetype outputs from a single brief — Mover, Validator, Explorer

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan — use sparingly (one per pricing section).

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, token config, palette map, IA planner, and multi-variant generation. Yours forever — no subscription, no lock-in.
features: Semantic resolution engine — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, context mode
IA planner + multi-variant generation agent (Claude Opus)
Three palette presets — funnel-driven, archetype-driven, feature-emphasis
Token dashboard with editable palette map
Lifetime access — runs in your codebase, not a vendor dashboard

page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). centered variant appropriate for terminal conversion action.

slots.headline: The intelligence layer is ready. So is the decision.
slots.subhead: MODE ships as a code kit — running in under an hour, configured once, owned permanently. No subscription to justify, no vendor dependency to manage.
slots.trust_signal: Lifetime access. No subscription. No credit card stored after purchase.

page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.
